metadata.title: United States Language Diffusion Model
metadata.description: Master configuration object for a Mapbox + D3 visualization of language spread in the United States.
metadata.data_note: This package is map-ready but conceptually illustrative. Replace illustrative intensity_score and strength values with measured corpus, acoustic, survey, or field-interview data before presenting it as empirical research.

map_explanation_for_users: Large circles show where a language feature is strongest. Lines show likely diffusion paths between connected cities. Transparent regions show broad dialect zones. The timeline shows when a feature appears, intensifies, or spreads.

core_principles.summary: Language diffusion often follows communication networks rather than simple physical distance.
Dialect regions should be represented as soft zones, not hard borders.
Phonetic shifts, phonetic convergence, and lexical diffusion should be filterable because they spread at different speeds.
Points should represent observed or sampled locations. Polygons should provide context, not overstate precision.
